subject: Biology
title: Probiotics and Prebiotics
description: A quick, practical guide to Probiotics and Prebiotics.

hook.text: Probiotics are live microbes you consume to benefit your gut. Prebiotics feed the ones already there.

buildup.text: Yogurt, kimchi, and sauerkraut contain live bacteria cultures that may support gut health.

discovery.text: Prebiotics are fibers like inulin that pass through your stomach and feed beneficial gut bacteria.

twist.text: Most probiotic supplements lack strong clinical evidence. The strains and doses matter enormously.

climax.text: Fecal transplants—transferring a healthy microbiome—have cured severe gut infections.

punchline.text: Feed your microbes well. They'll feed you back.

quiz.question: What is the difference between probiotics and prebiotics?
options: Probiotics are live microbes; prebiotics feed them
Prebiotics are live microbes; probiotics feed them
